eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Valerio Aboian at 50% implied probability for their ATP Challenger Santos round-of-32 clash on clay, capturing the competitive balance between the No. 452-ranked Argentine and No. 424 Brazilian Pedro Sakamoto, who holds a 1-0 head-to-head edge from a grueling three-set win (6-7, 7-6, 6-4) last June in Santa Cruz. Both arrive off recent clay-court losses—Sakamoto 1-2 to Juan Bautista Torres on April 15 and Aboian 0-2 to Hugo Dellien on April 17—amid middling 2026 records of 17-13 and 7-10, respectively. Sakamoto's home-crowd boost in Santos could tip scales, while Aboian's endurance in long rallies poses upset threat; no injury concerns reported, but weather or late scratches remain variables.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos first-round clash on clay, No. 407 Eduardo Ribeiro enters as the ranking favorite over No. 501 Miguel Tobon, bolstered by his superior career clay record (200-132) and home-country advantage in Brazil. eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos on clay, Argentine Facundo Mena (#290) faces Brazilian veteran Daniel Dutra da Silva (#382) in a first-round matchup, marking their 10th head-to-head encounter where Mena leads 5-4 overall. markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both ZennIT and Dynasty each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dynasty enters this Road of Legends Spring regular season BO3 with a 2-3 series record and 5-8 games in fifth place, positioning them for playoffs ahead of struggling ZennIT at 1-4 series and 3-9 games in last place. ZennIT's only win was a 2-1 over Myth Esports on April 21, but they've since lost 0-2 to Senshi eSports on April 30 amid a four-loss streak overall, highlighting macro execution issues. Dynasty snapped early wins over mCon and Myth before dropping 1-2 to Once Upon A Team and 0-2 to Frites last week, yet boasts a recent 1-0 head-to-head win over ZennIT from Winter 2026 seeding. Stable rosters and no reported changes leave draft synergy and early skirmishes as key differentiators in the current patch.

Dynasty snapped early wins over mCon and Myth before dropping 1-2 to Once Upon A Team and 0-2 to Frites last week, yet boasts a recent 1-0 head-to-head win over ZennIT from Winter 2026 seeding. Iga Świątek sits second at 20.0% after dropping to No. 4 in WTA rankings, her odds tempered by a third-round retirement due to a gastrointestinal virus against Ann Li in Madrid, raising fitness concerns ahead of the main draw. Elena Rybakina's 13.9% reflects her No. 2 ranking and Stuttgart clay title earlier in April, while Coco Gauff at 8.5% trails as defending champion amid inconsistent recent clay form, with Mirra Andreeva's Madrid final run boosting her to 7.5%. Rome remains ongoing, potentially shifting sentiment further.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos on clay, Argentine Facundo Mena (#290) faces Brazilian veteran Daniel Dutra da Silva (#382) in a first-round matchup, marking their 10th head-to-head encounter where Mena leads 5-4 overall. Dutra da Silva enters with momentum from a decisive 6-2, 6-2 straight-sets victory over Mena in the second round of last month's Campinas Challenger on similar clay, snapping Mena's prior three-match winning streak against him. Both are clay specialists with strong surface histories, though Mena's higher ranking and recent Challenger form give him an edge, tempered by Dutra's home-crowd support and left-handed game. No injuries reported; typical coastal weather expected, with no late withdrawals noted. Trader consensus reflects a competitive bout amid uncertain momentum shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yankees right-hander Cam Schlittler commands trader consensus for the 2026 AL Cy Young at 27% implied probability, propelled by his MLB-leading xERA, xFIP, strikeout rates, and fWAR through early May, alongside a 1.51 ERA, 0.74 WHIP, and 49 Ks over 41.2 innings in eight starts—tweaked cutter velocity and increased sinker usage fueling dominance. Tarik Skubal's recent injury announcement, dropping him from favoritism, widened the field, boosting Schlittler ahead of teammate Max Fried (12.9%), whose dream start features top-tier peripherals in Yankee Stadium. Angels' Jose Soriano (10.4%) differentiates with a 1.74 ERA and 54 Ks, while Dylan Cease (7.6%) leverages strikeout prowess and Parker Messick (7.3%) shines via Guardians rotation stability despite a recent stumble—highlighting a competitive mix of breakouts, health, and workload sustainability.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Mexico announce an increase at the June meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's June 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for June 25, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their June meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Mexico Decision in June

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 84% implied probability to no change in Banco de México's (Banxico) target overnight interbank rate at its June 2026 meeting, following the surprise 25 basis point cut to 6.75% on March 26 amid a divided Governing Board vote and despite persistent inflation above the 3% target. Early April 2026 headline CPI eased to 4.53% year-on-year from prior highs, signaling modest disinflation that supports a potential pause after the expected May 7 rate reduction priced at over 90% in parallel markets. Governor remarks on March 30 highlighted the easing cycle nearing completion, with core price pressures and rising global yields reinforcing trader caution for further adjustments. The May decision remains the pivotal catalyst ahead.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 35°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Emerging El Niño patterns amplify regional warmth above May's climatological average of 32°C, but model discrepancies on shower timing create genuine uncertainty, reflected in tightly clustered market-implied probabilities; watch METMalaysia's daily update on May 6 for refined guidance.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping purge Wang Huning in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions are met by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

1) The listed individual is removed or resigns from their primary political post or from their position, if any, on the Chinese Politburo or its standing committee, with a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor.

2) The listed individual is expelled from the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). 

Qualifying announcements and reporting of a purge before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal/expulsion goes into effect.

A listed individual leaving office at the end of a regularly scheduled term, or being removed/resigning from office without a consensus of credible reporting describing the resignation/removal as a “purge”, “ousting”, or similar language, or to have presumably or definitively occurred as a result of corruption, bribery, other criminal wrongdoing, or a lack of political favor, will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from the Chinese Government and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's anti-corruption campaign has intensified in 2026, with purges extending from the People's Liberation Army (PLA) high command to the Politburo, signaling unrelenting efforts to enforce political loyalty amid leadership vacancies. Key developments include the January ouster of top CMC Vice Chairman Zhang Youxia—a longtime ally—and Joint Staff Chief Liu Zhenli, followed by additional senior generals ahead of the March Two Sessions and, most recently, Politburo member Ma Xingrui in early April for "severe violations," marking the third such case this year. These moves, investigated by the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection (CCDI), have decimated PLA ranks, particularly in the Rocket Force, leaving command gaps. Traders watch for further CCDI probes or announcements before year-end, as no senior figure appears immune.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: INFINITE enters the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal as the clear favorite at HLTV world rank #59, fresh off a United21 League Season 46 grand final appearance against BIG Academy, with a stable lineup led by kreaz, Blytz, and mhN1 showcasing consistent midround execution and pistol round strength. Imperial Academy (#193), featuring a revamped roster since March—Aliot, Kragh, aZe, ta9z, and sh1Fu (avg age 18.9)—builds momentum from 3rd place in Season 45, dominating Ancient (75% win rate) and Nuke while exposing vulnerabilities on Mirage. No prior head-to-head; BO3 outcome likely turns on vetoes, Imperial's academy synergy versus INFINITE's experience, and CS2 meta adaptations amid stable patch conditions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 84-85°F on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Houston high temperature of 84-85°F (35.5% implied probability) or 86-87°F (25.5%), aligning with National Weather Service (NWS) forecast models showing partly sunny conditions and highs near 85°F on May 6 amid building high pressure over Texas. Recent heavy rainfall through May 1—dropping highs to the 70s and saturating soils—has introduced lingering cloud cover and evaporative cooling, capping extremes below 90°F per GFS and European model ensembles, though dry air advection could nudge peaks higher. Climatological normals for early May at Houston's Intercontinental Airport hover around 84°F, with low wind shear supporting these mid-80s outcomes. Watch NWS updates and 12z model runs on May 5 for refinements ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors a highest temperature of 64-65°F in Los Angeles (85.5% implied probability), aligning with National Weather Service forecasts projecting peaks near 65°F at coastal stations like LAX amid persistent marine layer coverage. Recent observations confirm cool conditions, with May 4 highs at 68°F giving way to morning lows around 57°F on May 5 and daytime readings hovering 63-66°F under extensive stratus clouds and west-southwest onshore winds of 10-15 mph advecting chilly Pacific air. This "May Gray" pattern, common in early May climatology where average highs reach 74°F, has lingered over the past week due to steady low-pressure troughing aloft, limiting solar insolation and convective clearing. Model consensus shows low confidence in burn-off today, though unexpected thinning clouds could push toward 66-67°F (15% odds); track real-time Wunderground data at USC Downtown for resolution.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 65-89 tweets from May 7 to May 9, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 7 12:00 PM ET to May 9, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Kawasaki Frontale vs. Tōkyō Verdy: O/U 2.5

markets.question: Will Trump reduce the deficit before 2027?
markets.description:
The U.S. federal budget deficit for September 2025 was $197,949,630,362.16 (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) reports a lower monthly deficit in December 2026 than in September 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source will be the Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS) published by the U.S. Department of the Treasury (fiscaldata.treasury.gov). The month surplus can be found in the column labeled "Current Month Deficit Surplus Amount" in the the table "Summary of Receipts, Outlays, and Surplus or Deficit” in the MTS (see: https://fiscaldata.treasury.gov/datasets/monthly-treasury-statement/summary-of-receipts-outlays-and-the-deficit-surplus-of-the-u-s-government).  If no report is published by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET another credible source will be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 84.5% implied probability for "No," driven by Congressional Budget Office projections showing the fiscal year 2026 federal deficit at $1.9 trillion (5.8% of GDP), with year-to-date figures already exceeding $1 trillion through February amid persistent revenue shortfalls and spending pressures. President Trump's policy mix—extension of 2017 tax cuts adding $1.4 trillion to decade-long deficits, alongside 42% defense spending hikes to $1.5 trillion in the April FY2027 budget proposal—offsets proposed non-defense discretionary cuts, per CBO analysis. Recent tariff policy shifts could further widen shortfalls by $1.1 trillion over ten years, reinforcing fiscal expansion. Key catalysts include final FY2026 Treasury data by September and debt ceiling negotiations.

markets.question: Will Tencent have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank among primarily Chinese companies, based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Qualifying Chinese Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Meituan” would be ranked ahead of “Xiaomi”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Matteo Arnaldi vs Jaume Munar
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Matteo Arnaldi and Jaume Munar in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matteo Arnaldi' if Matteo Arnaldi advances against Jaume Munar.

This market will resolve to 'Jaume Munar' if Jaume Munar advances against Matteo Arnaldi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Matteo Arnaldi enters as the 74% trader-favored home wildcard at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, buoyed by his recent Cagliari Challenger clay title—his career-most significant triumph—where he saved a match point en route to the semis before likely clinching the hardware, injecting vital match minutes ahead of Rome. Ranked No. 106 with spotty ATP-level results this year, Arnaldi leverages Foro Italico crowd support and clay comfort against No. 38 Jaume Munar, who grinded through qualifiers post right-arm injury recovery, endured a dismal Madrid Masters exit to Casper Ruud (0-6, 1-6), and holds a 3-2 head-to-head edge mostly on hard courts. The first-round clash sets up a potential second-round vs. sixth seed Alex de Minaur.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Israel as the narrow televote frontrunner for Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, driven by Noam Bettan's up-tempo "Michelle"—a rhythmic pop track with broad international appeal and strong betting odds reflecting diaspora voting blocs amid ongoing participation controversy. Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen follow closely with "Liekinheitin," topping fan polls like "My Eurovision 2026" and gaining momentum from Nordic charisma and pre-rehearsal buzz, while Greece's Akylas vaults into contention via the competitive Sing for Greece win with energetic "Ferto," bolstered by OGAE points. With rehearsals underway ahead of May 12-16 semis and final, staging reveals and live performances could swing this razor-thin top-three battle, where public sentiment favors catchy anthems over jury bait.

markets.question: Will Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) hit (HIGH) $88 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Rocket Lab USA, Inc. (RKLB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.RKLB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Senator Lindsey Graham leads trader consensus at 86.5% implied probability to win South Carolina's June 9 Republican Senate primary, bolstered by his massive campaign war chest, presidential endorsement from Donald Trump, and historical incumbency advantages in the deep-red state. Challenger Mark Lynch holds 11.9% following Paul Dans' April 10 dropout and endorsement, which consolidated anti-Graham votes from the Project 2025 architect amid polls showing Graham below the 50% threshold needed to avoid a potential runoff. Low shares for Paul Dans (0.8%) and Thomas Murphy (0.1%) reflect their limited resources and name recognition as the primary nears, with traders betting on Graham's institutional edge despite right-wing media buzz around Lynch.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 280-299 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Tarik Skubal strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 81.5% implied probability to no Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, reflecting the lack of a public S-1 filing since the company's confidential submission in January amid a missed March debut target. Secondary market valuations have compressed to $7-10 billion—down sharply from $15 billion peaks—due to monetization challenges in Discord's freemium model serving a gaming-heavy user base, slower revenue growth around $800 million annually versus enterprise rivals like Slack's $2.5 billion, and broader SaaS multiple contraction. The <15B market cap outcome at 15.7% captures residual pricing for a subdued debut if executed, with higher brackets near negligible amid competitive pressures; traders eye any S-1 disclosure as the pivotal near-term catalyst against a compressing timeline.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yankees right-hander Cam Schlittler commands trader consensus for the 2026 AL Cy Young at 27% implied probability, propelled by his MLB-leading xERA, xFIP, strikeout rates, and fWAR through early May, alongside a 1.51 ERA, 0.74 WHIP, and 49 Ks over 41.2 innings in eight starts—tweaked cutter velocity and increased sinker usage fueling dominance. Tarik Skubal's recent injury announcement, dropping him from favoritism, widened the field, boosting Schlittler ahead of teammate Max Fried (12.9%), whose dream start features top-tier peripherals in Yankee Stadium. Angels' Jose Soriano (10.4%) differentiates with a 1.74 ERA and 54 Ks, while Dylan Cease (7.6%) leverages strikeout prowess and Parker Messick (7.3%) shines via Guardians rotation stability despite a recent stumble—highlighting a competitive mix of breakouts, health, and workload sustainability.

markets.question: Billions FDV above $50M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Billion's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Billions (https://x.com/billions_ntwk) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Stefanos Tsitsipas enters as the 63.5% implied probability favorite against Tomas Machac in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia Round 1, reflecting his superior clay-court pedigree and recent form resurgence despite a shaky start to the European swing. Tsitsipas, a three-time Monte Carlo Masters champion and past Rome finalist, notched a confidence-boosting third-round appearance in Madrid last week by defeating eighth seed Alexander Bublik 6-2, 7-5—his first top-20 clay win since the Paris Olympics—while declaring himself physically recovered from prior back issues. Machac, who stunned Tsitsipas in four sets at the Australian Open on hard courts (leveling their 1-1 head-to-head), showed clay promise by pushing Jannik Sinner to three sets in Monte Carlo's Round of 16 but holds a mixed 4-4 clay record this season. Traders price in Tsitsipas' surface expertise and Foro Italico experience amid a competitive matchup on outdoor red clay.

Gibson's momentum from a Miami Open round of 16 run contrasts Trevisan's post-foot surgery recovery, where she's 9-7 YTD and 5-3 on recent ITF clay but 0-7 in her last eight main-draw clay matches. No head-to-head history exists, but Trevisan's career 238-130 clay edge and home crowd provide upset potential amid Gibson's limited clay experience, including a Saint-Malo first-round exit last week. No injury concerns reported pre-match.

Tottenham Hotspur (17th, 37 points) escaped danger with consecutive victories—a 1-0 at Wolves and 2-1 at Aston Villa—boosting trader confidence in their three remaining fixtures: home vs Leeds, away at Chelsea, and home vs Everton. Nottingham Forest (16th, 42 points), six points clear, sit safest after winning four of five, including 3-1 at Chelsea, though they face Chelsea away, Newcastle home, Manchester United away, and Bournemouth home. West Ham's tougher run-in—Arsenal home, Newcastle away, Leeds home—coupled with a league-worst -19 goal difference, drives the tight consensus on the final relegation spot with three matchweeks left.

markets.question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Opendoor is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Opendoor's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.10 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Opendoor reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.10 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Opendoor releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will Zelenskyy post 0-19 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Volodymyr Zelenskyy (@ZelenskyyUa), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

markets.question: Tesla (TSLA) Up or Down on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on May 5, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on May 5, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 18°C or higher on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official observations from the Milano Malpensa International Airport station (LIMC), the market's resolution source via Weather Underground, confirm a daily high temperature of exactly 16°C on May 5, 2026, amid persistent overcast skies, intermittent showers, and a fast-moving cold front suppressing daytime heating. This aligns precisely with ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecast consensus, which predicted subdued peaks below 17°C following prior days' 25–26°C highs, driven by unstable northwesterly flow and reduced solar insolation. Trader sentiment at 100% implied probability for 16°C reflects locked-in hourly METAR data with minimal daylight remaining for warming. Realistic challenges include rare data revisions or an overlooked peak reading, though improbable given consistent regional monitoring from Italy's Aeronautica Militare.

markets.question: Crystal Palace FC vs. FK Shakhtar Donetsk: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Europa Conference League game between Crystal Palace FC and FK Shakhtar Donetsk, scheduled for May 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Crystal Palace FC and FK Shakhtar Donetsk combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Crystal Palace FC vs. FK Shakhtar Donetsk - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Europa Conference League game, scheduled for May 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace hosts Shakhtar Donetsk in the UEFA Europa Conference League semi-final second leg on May 7 at Selhurst Park, following the first leg on neutral turf in Krakow on April 30, where Shakhtar's home games are played amid ongoing conflict. Both sides advanced convincingly in quarters—Palace edging Fiorentina 4-2 aggregate despite a 2-1 second-leg loss, Shakhtar dominating AZ Alkmaar 5-2 overall—with recent domestic form bolstering confidence: Shakhtar's 2-1 win at Zorya on April 23 caps three straight victories as Ukrainian Premier League leaders, while mid-table Palace drew West Ham on April 20 after a dramatic 2-1 stoppage-time triumph over Newcastle. Key concerns include Palace's season-ending hamstring injury to striker Eddie Nketiah and doubts over Adam Wharton and Maxence Lacroix, versus Shakhtar midfielder Isaque's recovery from a head knock against AZ; home advantage and Glasner's tactical setup could prove decisive in this evenly poised tie.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's insurmountable 33-goal lead in the Bundesliga top goalscorer race, 15 ahead of VfB Stuttgart's Deniz Undav on 18 after 32 matchdays, has solidified trader consensus at 99.6% implied probability. Bayern Munich, atop the table with 83 points, have powered Kane's record-breaking campaign through consistent finishing, including recent strikes amid their title push. No injury concerns currently hinder the England captain, whose pace outstrips Europe's top leagues. With two matchdays left—featuring winnable fixtures for Bayern—challengers like Patrik Schick (16 goals) or Serhou Guirassy (15) face mathematical impossibility without extraordinary hauls. Only a severe Kane injury or unprecedented upset scoring from rivals could shift odds, scenarios traders deem negligible given the gap and form.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

This fragmented positioning stems from last week's reports spotlighting high-profile candidacies—Navalnaya's rising profile as heir to Alexei Navalny's anti-Putin resistance, Trump's nominations from Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan leaders for brokered diplomacy, and Zelenskyy's early April endorsement by an Oslo professor alongside Ukraine's people for defiant wartime leadership. markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MA-07 House seat?

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between The Soulless Team and Kings Crown in the BetBoom Rise of Legends 2nd Division, initially scheduled for May 4 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "The Soulless Team" if The Soulless Team win the match against Kings Crown.

This market will resolve to "Kings Crown" if Kings Crown win the match against The Soulless Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the BetBoom Rise of Legends Season 10 2nd Division double round-robin group stage, The Soulless Team co-leads standings at 4-1 match record (9-2 games, 13 points) after a clean 2-0 sweep of Aterion Esports on May 1, building on four straight prior wins marred only by a narrow 1-2 loss to Dark Phoenix on April 30. Kings Crown sits seventh at 2-3 (4-7 games, 5 points), salvaging momentum with a 2-1 comeback over The World on May 1 but hampered by 0-2 defeats to top contenders HARD GRIND, Team Spirit Academy White, and Aterion. TST boasts perfect win rates on key meta heroes like Khaleed, Harley, and Sora, while KC struggles on picks such as Zhuxin and Kalea; this BO3 clash carries weight for closed qualifier seeding in the B-Tier circuit.

markets.question: Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez vs. Jose Pereira: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez and Jose Pereira in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Santos: Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez vs Jose Pereira Total Sets: O/U 2.5

events.title: Santos: Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez vs Jose Pereira
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez and Jose Pereira in the Santos, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez' if Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez advances against Jose Pereira.

This market will resolve to 'Jose Pereira' if Jose Pereira advances against Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Santos on clay, trader consensus reflects a razor-thin 50% implied probability for Lorenzo Joaquin Rodriguez over qualifier Jose Pereira, balancing Rodriguez's superior current ranking (No. 459 vs. No. 715) and recent ITF form—including a straight-sets win over Enzo Camargo Lima last week—with Pereira's veteran experience (career-high No. 232), home-crowd edge as a Brazilian, and successful qualification run through the Santos prelims. No prior head-to-head exists, amplifying uncertainty on this outdoor clay surface where both South Americans thrive. Late scratches, weather delays, or pre-match warmups could sway odds, as challengers often see stylistic clay-court battles turn on break-point conversion and endurance.

markets.question: Will Anthropic flip BTC by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the public valuation of Anthropic surpasses the market capitalization of Bitcoin (BTC) at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For Bitcoin, CoinGecko's data for BTC will be used, currently available at https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/bitcoin. If CoinGecko stops showing relevant data, data from CoinMarketCap will be used instead.

For Anthropic, any public valuation, or private funding round that values the company will be considered. To qualify, the valuation must be explicitly confirmed by Anthropic or an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. 

The resolution source will CoinGecko and Anthropic's official communications; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will "iloveitiloveitiloveit - Bella Kay" be the #1 song on US Spotify this week?
markets.description:
Spotify curates a playlist of the most streamed songs globally and updates it on Fridays to reflect streaming data for the previous week, beginning on the preceding Friday and ending on Thursday.

This market will resolve according to the most-streamed song in the U.S. on Spotify for the week labeled May 8.

If Spotify does not release its top song for the week labeled May 8 by May 9, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will default to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Spotify. The weekly top songs - USA chart can be found on open.spotify.com under the "Charts" heading.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas," a country crossover juggernaut with over 200 days on Spotify charts and multiple prior US #1 weeks, commands 76% trader consensus as the frontrunner for US Spotify weekly #1 ending May 8, driven by its sustained 1.7 million+ daily streams amid historic Hot 100 dominance. Olivia Rodrigo's surging single "drop dead" trails at 22% implied odds after reclaiming daily #1 yesterday (May 4) with 1.87 million streams versus Ella's 1.71 million—its eighth such day—but cumulative weekly totals favor the veteran's consistency over the newer track's peaks. Lower contenders like Dominic Fike's "Babydoll" (daily #4) and Noah Kahan's "Doors" lack comparable volume. Final dailies through May 8 hold swing potential amid streaming volatility.

If this tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a winner has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizer, ESL (https://pro.eslgaming.com/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

Falcons sit at 12.5% as primary challengers after snapping Vitality's 18-map win streak with a 2-1 group stage upset at Rio, powered by NiKo and m0NESY despite kyxsan departure rumors. Natus Vincere (6.3%) and Team Spirit (5.5%) hold strong Stage 3 seeding from Valve Regional Standings, alongside FURIA and PARIVISION, setting up Swiss round-robin battles into single-elimination playoffs at Lanxess Arena.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding victory from pole at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix, his third consecutive win after China and Japan, has extended his drivers' championship lead to 100 points over Mercedes teammate George Russell's 80, solidifying trader consensus at 40.4% and 26.5% implied probabilities respectively. Mercedes' early-season dominance, with consistent podiums and superior race pace, has positioned the duo as frontrunners amid Red Bull and McLaren struggles—Max Verstappen languishes outside the top five while Lando Norris holds third in markets at 11.1% despite fourth in standings. Recent grid penalties and Ferrari's post-Miami woes for Charles Leclerc further cap challengers, though long-term consistency and evolving regulations loom as variables.

markets.question: US x Cuba military clash in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Cuba between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Cuban military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the United States Coast Guard is considered part of the United States military, and the Cuban Border Guard is considered part of the Cuban military.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward no US-Cuba military clash in 2026 at 56%, driven by ongoing bilateral diplomacy amid President Trump's escalatory rhetoric and sanctions. Recent US official visits to Havana in late April proposed Cuban reforms in exchange for sanctions relief on oil shipments, signaling a preferred off-ramp despite Pentagon contingency planning reported April 15 and Trump's May 1 remarks hinting at an "immediate" takeover via naval presence. Cuba has denounced these as "unprecedented threats" while confirming talks, with no troop mobilizations or airstrikes observed. Senate rejection of curbs on executive military authority last week sustains uncertainty, but traders weigh high intervention costs against negotiation progress and post-Iran war priorities as factors favoring de-escalation.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from AtlasIntel, Nexus, Quaest, and Datafolha in late April 2026 consistently place President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva ahead in first-round voting intentions at 37–47%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro—endorsed by his ineligible father, former President Jair Bolsonaro—solidly second at 30–40%, consolidating the right-wing vote after São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas opted for re-election. This positioning reflects Flávio's rapid gains amid a fragmented field, where contenders like Ronaldo Caiado and Romeu Zema poll under 6%, driving trader consensus to 63% implied probability for his second-place finish ahead of the October 4 first round. Lula's lower 20% odds for second stem from his incumbency edge and lead in multi-candidate scenarios, though tightening runoffs signal uncertainty. PT alternatives like Camilo Santana trail due to weak showings.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruno Fernandes leads Polymarket's trader consensus at 64% implied probability for 2025/26 PFA Players' Player of the Year thanks to his Premier League-leading 19 assists—nearing the single-season record—paired with 8 goals, fueling Manchester United's surge to third in the table amid a strong finish. Declan Rice sits at 24% as Arsenal's midfield engine, with 4 goals and 7-8 assists anchoring the Gunners' five-point title lead over Manchester City, solidified yesterday by City's 3-3 draw at Everton. Erling Haaland's top-scorer status (23+ goals) holds at 9.8% despite City's faltering goal difference, while William Saliba's defensive solidity bolsters Arsenal's backline at 5.9%; recent title-race tension and Fernandes' creative dominance have widened his edge over challengers in player-voted balloting.

markets.question: FaZe roster change Before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves FaZe Clan’s active Counter-Strike 2 roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the FaZe Clan Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/FaZe_Clan)  before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: broky, frozen, jcobbb, and Twistzz. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

If no qualifying roster change is confirmed at the time GTA VI is released, this market will resolve to “No” immediately.

For the purposes of this market, “release” refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the United States. Early access, beta versions, or leaks do not qualify. A release limited to specific platforms (e.g., console-only) will qualify.

The primary resolution sources will be official information from Liquipedia, Rockstar Games, or Take-Two Interactive; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: FaZe Clan's CS2 roster turmoil drives the 85% implied probability for a change before GTA VI's November 19 release, as traders price in ongoing instability from a dismal 2026 start. Legendary IGL karrigan departed for Team Falcons in late April after FaZe missed the latest Major via a shocking VRS collapse, prompting Neityu as stand-in with Twistzz trialing IGL duties. New coach Niclas “enkay J” Krumhorn replaced NEO amid whispers of frozen's exit and a potential rebuild targeting talents like woxic and mail09. Subpar form—few wins against top teams—fuels expectations of further roster moves in the crowded transfer window before the distant deadline.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé leads the UEFA Champions League top scorer race with 15 goals for Real Madrid, ahead of Harry Kane's 13 for Bayern Munich, positioning traders to price Mbappé as a 57% implied probability favorite despite Real Madrid's quarterfinal exit to Bayern. Kane has surged recently, netting in both legs against Madrid and adding a goal plus assist in Bayern's semifinal first leg versus PSG three days ago, keeping the race tight as Bayern remains alive with potential final spots ahead. Others like Khvicha Kvaratskhelia trail at 10 goals but with lower probabilities due to fewer remaining fixtures and team paths, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in this late-stage duel.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 76% implied probability to no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, anchored by the April 29 FOMC decision to hold the federal funds target at 3.50%-3.75% despite elevated PCE inflation projections near 3% from oil-driven geopolitical shocks like the Iran conflict. March CPI surged 1.1% month-over-month, yet stable unemployment at 4.3% and resilient growth have tempered hike urgency, with the March dot plot median envisioning steady or modestly lower rates through year-end. Dissent in April highlighted easing risks over tightening, aligning with CME FedWatch pricing low hike odds through September. Key catalysts include April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPY) hit (HIGH) $780 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPY) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the S&P 500 (SPY) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.SPY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPY) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 stands near record highs around 7,230 as of May 5, 2026, with SPY trading at approximately $721–$724 following an all-time high of 7,272 on May 1, reflecting year-to-date gains of over 5% driven by robust Q1 earnings beats from major constituents. The Federal Reserve held the fed funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75% in its April 29 meeting amid March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, tempering rate-cut expectations despite some internal dissent. Trader sentiment hinges on upcoming catalysts including the May 6–7 FOMC meeting, April nonfarm payrolls on May 8, and CPI data on May 12, alongside ongoing earnings reports and Middle East geopolitical tensions that could spur volatility.

markets.question: Will Anthony Hudson win the 2026 Michigan Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
