eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors self-funded businessman Perry Johnson at 65% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, 2026, ahead of U.S. Rep. John James at 32%, reflecting bets on Johnson's aggressive ad spending and appeal to the MAGA base despite recent polls. A Detroit Regional Chamber/Glengariff survey from late April showed James leading 37%-20% among likely primary voters, with former AG Mike Cox at 10%, but traders anticipate Johnson's heavy self-financing—echoing his near-frontrunner status in 2022 before a signature scandal—will boost turnout among core Republicans in this low-information primary. Evan Space garners minor support as a perennial challenger, while field fragmentation aids the top two; upcoming candidate filings and debates could shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Capitão Wagner win the 2026 Ceará gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Ceará gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Genial/Quaest polling from late April highlights the tight Ceará gubernatorial contest ahead of the October 4 first round and potential runoff, showing Ciro Gomes leading incumbent Elmano de Freitas 41%-32% in first-round matchups while trailing senator Camilo Santana 39%-44% in a second-round scenario. Trader consensus reflects this, with Ciro at 43% implied probability due to his consistent lead across surveys like Datafolha and Paraná Pesquisas, but Camilo's 36% pricing captures PT speculation on replacing Elmano to bolster runoff chances. Eduardo Girão's 28% underscores right-wing consolidation potential despite sub-10% polls. PT nominee dynamics, endorsements, and national polarization could tip the balance in this closely contested race.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories fell sharply by 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the EIA's latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report for the week ending April 24, driven by record exports of 6.44 million barrels per day and refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day. Stocks remain about 1% above the five-year seasonal average despite prior weeks' builds. The Strategic Petroleum Reserve holds 398 million barrels following recent drawdowns. Key factors include steady Permian Basin production near 13.3 million barrels per day, declining imports, and ramping summer gasoline demand. Upcoming EIA reports on May 6, 13, 20, 27, and June 3 will track trends through the week ending June 5, alongside global supply risks from Middle East tensions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 35°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: India Meteorological Department observations at Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport confirm Lucknow's maximum temperature on May 5 reached just 28°C—11.4°C below the climatological normal for early May—amid persistent partly cloudy skies, thundery developments, and isolated rain showers that suppressed daytime heating. Preceding forecasts from IMD projected highs of 28–31°C under these pre-monsoon disturbances, aligning with model consensus on cooler air masses overriding typical heat buildup. This verified observational data drives the market's 100% implied probability for "33°C or below," reflecting trader consensus on authoritative station measurements. Realistic challenges include rare post-day data revisions or instrument anomalies, though standardized protocols minimize such risks as resolution nears.

markets.question: Will Trump meet with Kim Jong Un in May 2026?

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's confirmed White House visit this Thursday, announced May 4 amid talks on Venezuela policy, trade tariffs, and regional security, represents the freshest catalyst elevating trader consensus for high-profile May meetings. This pairs with President Trump's longstanding Beijing summit schedule versus Chinese President Xi Jinping on May 14-15—rescheduled in late March post-Iran de-escalation and reaffirmed despite US accusations of Chinese funding for Iranian proxies via oil purchases and Taiwan frictions. Secondary contenders like Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu or Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman depend on Middle East ceasefires, while domestic figures such as Elon Musk factor into routine interactions. Markets track verifiable in-person encounters through May 31 per credible reports, with diplomatic surprises from Ukraine or Gaza as key risks to shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes leads at 61% implied probability as the Chiefs' Week 1 starting QB for 2026, fueled by recent ACL rehab updates showing him ahead of schedule—throwing passes and on track for OTAs after December 2025 surgery—bolstering trader confidence in his timely return despite typical nine-month timelines. Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. Chris Oladokun's 24.9% reflects his entrenched practice squad role and offseason buzz as a surprising contender over Fields, per mid-April reports, while veterans Joe Flacco (18.8%) and Gardner Minshew (8.1%) linger as free-agent fallback options amid roster depth including draft pick Garrett Nussmeier.

markets.question: Will the Detroit Pistons win the NBA Eastern Conference Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Detroit Pistons win the 2025–2026 NBA Eastern Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025–26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals based on the rules of the NBA.

If the 2025-26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals winner is not announced by June 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Knicks lead trader consensus at 43.5% implied probability for Eastern Conference champion after a dominant 137-98 Game 1 rout of the Philadelphia 76ers in their semifinals series, with Jalen Brunson erupting for 35 points amid a historic 47-point halftime lead that underscores New York's playoff momentum from their first-round 4-2 dismissal of Atlanta. Detroit Pistons hold 27.2% as the No. 1 seed hosting Cleveland Cavaliers (22.5%) in a 0-0 series opener Tuesday, both teams fatigued from grueling Game 7 first-round triumphs—Pistons rallying from 3-1 down vs. Orlando Magic, Cavs edging Toronto Raptors—highlighting Detroit's elite defense and home-court edge against Cleveland's potent attack. 76ers trail at 8.6% post-blowout, despite their upset of Boston. Minimal injury concerns persist across matchups.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin hit $70k or $90k first?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the lower price in the title if Bitcoin’s price dips to that level or below before it hits the higher title price between the creation of this market and December 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. It will resolve to the higher price in the title if Bitcoin’s price first reaches that level or above before it dips to the lower title price during the same period.

If neither price level is reached within the market timeframe, the market will resolve 50–50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" and "Low" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Slovenia

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22 parliamentary election, which produced a hung National Assembly with Robert Golob's Freedom Movement holding 29 seats and Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28, outgoing PM Golob conceded failure to form a coalition on April 20 after talks collapsed with centre-right and other parties. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister-designate on April 25 amid lacking majority support, shifting momentum to SDS-led negotiations. Trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability for Janša reflects his May 5 announcement of agreed coalition guidelines with potential partners like the NSi-SLS-FOKUS bloc (9 seats), positioning him for parliamentary nomination starting May 6. Realistic challenges include stalled talks, partner defections such as from Democrats (6 seats) or Resni.ca (5 seats), or a presidential call for snap elections if no majority emerges by late 2026.

markets.question: Spread: RC Strasbourg Alsace (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "RC Strasbourg Alsace" if RC Strasbourg Alsace win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Angers SCO".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Angers SCO vs. RC Strasbourg Alsace - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace hold an advantageous position in the Ligue 1 table around 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, fueled by a dramatic 3-2 injury-time comeback victory at Lorient on April 26 that highlights their resilience amid a mixed run including losses to Rennes and Nice. Angers SCO, mired in 13th on 34 points from 31 games, struggle with poor recent form—drawing 1-1 versus Le Havre, losing 0-3 to PSG, and conceding heavily to Rennes and Lens—exacerbated by key injuries to Carlens Arcus (groin), Djibirin Harouna (ankle), and Yassin Belkhdim (arm). Strasbourg dominated earlier this season 5-0 at home, though Angers benefit from Stade Raymond Kopa home advantage and potential rest before matchday 33 on May 10. Trader consensus reflects Strasbourg's superior standing, momentum, and squad depth despite their own absences like Emanuel Emegha (muscle tear).

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.6% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone HICP inflation surged to 3.0% year-over-year in April 2026—its highest since September 2023—driven by spiking energy costs from Middle East tensions, lifting trader consensus toward higher annual 2026 averages as reflected in Polymarket's 41.4% implied probability for 3.1%+ and 27.8% for 2.8-3.0%. March's 2.6% print had already exceeded ECB staff projections of 2.6% for full-year 2026 HICP, with the latest Survey of Professional Forecasters revising expectations to 2.7% amid wage persistence and core measures at 2.4%. ECB held deposit facility rate at 2.0% on April 30 but signaled June hike risks via hawks like Nagel and Kazimir, tempering rate-cut bets; full April data due May 20 and May HICP could further shape pricing before year-end resolution.

Alphabet trails closely at 33.1%, fueled by a 10% stock rally following blowout Q1 2026 earnings, where Google Cloud revenue jumped 63% to over $20 billion, narrowing the gap to under $300 billion and sparking bets on further AI monetization gains. Other tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Tesla lag far behind with sub-2% odds due to slower growth trajectories, while Saudi Aramco's ~$1.8 trillion valuation remains detached from the AI-driven rally; upcoming earnings and chip shipment updates could sway the tight NVIDIA-Alphabet race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Assemblymember Alex Bores at 41% implied probability to win the NY-12 Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his campaign's April 17 poll showing a 38% lead among informed voters familiar with the top contenders, including Jack Schlossberg and Micah Lasher, amid 28% undecideds overall. Bores's support intensifies as name recognition grows, even after $2.4 million in super PAC attacks boosted his visibility. Lasher's 28% reflects Governor Hochul's April 13 endorsement and prior Bloomberg backing, while Schlossberg's 22% stems from Kennedy family name recognition in early March polls. A fresh AARP-Gotham analysis projects voters 50+ comprising 65-75% of turnout, favoring candidates addressing Social Security, Medicare, and affordability in this open seat replacing retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 82-83°F on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Houston high temperature of 84-85°F (35.5% implied probability) or 86-87°F (25.5%), aligning with National Weather Service (NWS) forecast models showing partly sunny conditions and highs near 85°F on May 6 amid building high pressure over Texas. Recent heavy rainfall through May 1—dropping highs to the 70s and saturating soils—has introduced lingering cloud cover and evaporative cooling, capping extremes below 90°F per GFS and European model ensembles, though dry air advection could nudge peaks higher. Climatological normals for early May at Houston's Intercontinental Airport hover around 84°F, with low wind shear supporting these mid-80s outcomes. Watch NWS updates and 12z model runs on May 5 for refinements ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's commanding 80% implied probability in the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary stems from his landslide endorsement at the April 26 state GOP convention, where he captured 70% of delegates—well above the 15% threshold for ballot qualification—while surging ahead in fundraising with nearly $10 million cash on hand to fuel statewide ads. Brian Shortsleeve narrowly cleared the 15% mark to secure primary ballot access, positioning him as the main challenger at 11.7%, amid calls for debates ahead of the September 1 primary. Mike Kennealy's slim 1.1% reflects his failure to qualify at the convention, limiting his path despite earlier polling leads, as trader consensus prioritizes party backing and resources in this low-turnout primary.

markets.question: LoL: Disguised vs Dignitas (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Disguised and Dignitas in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 9 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Disguised" if Disguised win the match against Dignitas.

This market will resolve to "Dignitas" if Dignitas win the match against Disguised.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Disguised enters this LCS regular season BO3 against Dignitas as the slight favorite among traders, reflecting their recent 2-0 upset win over DIG in Esports World Cup 2026 NA qualifiers that eliminated the opponent. Both squads languish at the bottom of Spring Split standings—Disguised with a 1-4 series record after a 0-3 loss to Team Liquid on May 2, and winless Dignitas reeling from sweeps by Shopify Rebellion and Cloud9 last week. Key factors include stagnant rosters, adaptation to the latest patch meta favoring aggressive early game, and desperation for series points to climb toward playoffs, with head-to-head history split but DSG's qualifier momentum tilting sentiment.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

Late scandals, opposition voter mobilization, or economic shocks could still narrow the gap before polls close.

markets.question: Will Aħwa Maltin win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 600-619 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini win the 2026 Men's French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a right wrist injury on April 24 has propelled Jannik Sinner to a dominant 70.5% implied probability as top seed and world No. 1, reflecting his unbeaten clay streak including five straight Masters 1000 titles and just two sets dropped over two months. Traders see Sinner's baseline consistency and mental edge on Roland Garros' clay surface as ideal for a career Grand Slam, especially with Novak Djokovic's shoulder injury clouding his preparation after a month sidelined. Alexander Zverev at 7.4% benefits from strong clay history and recent form, while Arthur Fils and Rafael Jodar gain from home-crowd support and upsets potential in a thinned field lacking other top contenders.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong consensus on a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2 trillion (54.5% implied probability) stems from the company's April 2026 confidential SEC filing and planned early June roadshow, targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation while raising up to $75 billion—elevating trader optimism beyond analyst targets amid Starlink's surging subscriber base and projected $15.5 billion 2025 revenue. Recent Starship Version 3 static-fire successes and Flight 12 targeting mid-May underscore reusable rocket milestones critical to Elon Musk's multiplanetary vision, justifying premium multiples versus peers. Lower brackets like 1.8T–2.0T (13%) reflect conservative views on execution risks, while "No IPO before 2028" languishes at 3.2% given firm 2026 timelines; watch Flight 12 and roadshow for sentiment shifts.

markets.question: Arsenal FC leading at halftime?
markets.description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026 If Arsenal FC wins within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: West Ham United FC vs. Arsenal FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between West Ham United FC and Arsenal FC, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 11:30 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices West Ham United FC, Draw, and Arsenal FC at 49.5¢ each for the halftime result, underscoring a fiercely competitive London derby where first-half caution prevails amid late-season stakes. West Ham, battling mid-table security with a gritty 2-1 home win over Everton on April 25 and a resilient 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace days earlier, leverage London Stadium's fortress-like atmosphere and solid home form against goal-shy opening periods. Title frontrunners Arsenal, who reclaimed Premier League summit via a narrow 1-0 victory versus Newcastle on the same weekend, face injury headwinds including Bukayo Saka's ankle issue, Martin Ødegaard's knee problem, and doubts over Declan Rice and Jurrien Timber, tempering their attacking fluency early. Head-to-head history shows tight first halves, amplifying the even-market dynamics.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 40-59 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Sen. Ted Cruz's X posts from May 5-12 remains tightly clustered across ranges, reflecting the variability in his prolific posting rate—historically around 20-30 daily as Congress's most active senator per Quorum analytics—driven by unpredictable Senate floor debates, Commerce Committee hearings, and travel. Recent weeks show steady output, with 10+ posts sampled from April 25-May 2 covering Iowa visits, Iran policy commentary, and Verdict podcast promotions, amid no major disruptions in the past 30 days. Separation could arise from scheduled Senate sessions mid-week boosting legislative reactions, foreign policy escalations like ceasefire talks, or personal events reducing volume; quiet recesses favor under 40, while news cycles push toward 140+.

markets.question: Will London have less than 5mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm at Heathrow (London Airport) in May, 2026, according to the Met Office.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Met Office, specifically the figure for May 2026 under "rain mm" at the https://www.metoffice.gov.uk/pub/data/weather/uk/climate/stationdata/heathrowdata.txt link once the Provisional figure for the whole month of May 2026 is released.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after Provisional data is released for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding performance in the April 16 special election for New Jersey's 11th Congressional District, succeeding Gov. Mikie Sherrill's vacancy, drives trader consensus at 96.7% odds for her 20-25% margin of victory over Republican Joe Hathaway. Final precinct-level tallies confirm her near-20-point edge through dominant wins in suburban Democratic strongholds, building on Sherrill's D+14 incumbency since flipping the district in 2018, bolstered by pre-election polls showing a strong lead and unified party support after a competitive primary. Low special election turnout favored the frontrunner. Official certification remains pending, but late absentee ballots, provisional vote adjustments, or counting discrepancies would need to erase over 3 points—unlikely absent legal challenges—to shift below 20%.

markets.question: Coinbase (COIN) Up or Down on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) on May 5, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) on May 5, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.COIN%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Surging Starlink revenue—projected at $22-24 billion for 2026 versus $15 billion in 2025—alongside reusable launch dominance and a February xAI merger, have propelled secondary tender valuations from $800 billion in December 2025 to $1.75-2 trillion IPO ambitions. Yet, sentiment tempers higher brackets like 80-90B (20.5%) due to execution risks in share allocation and market conditions, with resolution hinging on prospectus details and listing timeline amid robust secondary demand.

markets.question: Will Claudia López win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

events.title: Colombia Presidential Election
events.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors unidentified moderate Candidate M at 49.5% implied probability to win Colombia's May 31 presidential election, amid a fragmented field where polarization fatigue boosts centrist appeal over ideological extremes. Leftist Iván Cepeda Castro holds 36% on momentum from Pacto Histórico's strong March 8 parliamentary victories, securing Senate plurality despite President Petro's term limits. Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (32.5%) and Paloma Valencia (30%) split conservative support, with recent southwest bombings and assassinations elevating security concerns that favor tough stances, though April polls show Cepeda leading narrowly. Debates and voter turnout in battleground regions will likely determine first-round advancement and potential June runoff.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Paris be 8°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Météo-France guidance projects a minimum temperature of 11°C overnight into May 6 in Paris under cloudy skies, but international model ensembles like ECMWF and GFS show slightly cooler 9-10°C lows, fueling trader consensus split between those outcomes at 32.5% and 27.0% implied probabilities. Differentiating factors include extent of nocturnal cloud cover—persistent stratus layers insulating the surface to favor 10°C versus intermittent breaks enabling radiative cooling toward 9°C—amid light winds limiting turbulent mixing. Early May climatology supports this range, with historical lows averaging 9.5°C; new 12z model runs this afternoon could shift sentiment based on today's observed cooling trends.

markets.question: Fight to Go the Distance?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the fight between Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026, goes the full scheduled number of rounds and the result is determined by the judges' scorecards. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." 

Draws decided by the judges' scorecards after all scheduled rounds are completed will resolve "Yes." Technical decisions or technical draws declared before all rounds are completed will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 328: Sean Strickland vs. Khamzat Chimaev (Middleweight, Main Card)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Sean Strickland" if Sean Strickland is officially declared the winner of the fight against Khamzat Chimaev at UFC 328: Chimaev vs. Strickland, scheduled for May 9, 2026.

It will resolve to "Khamzat Chimaev" if Khamzat Chimaev is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 23, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: UFC 328's middleweight title main event pits undefeated champion Khamzat Chimaev (15-0) against durable challenger Sean Strickland (30-7) on May 9 at Newark's Prudential Center. Chimaev's relentless wrestling pressure and finishing power contrast Strickland's high-volume boxing, takedown defense, and late-round cardio, fueling trader consensus on a competitive five-round war. Fight week escalated with mutual trash talk threats prompting extra security and police presence, plus rumors of Chechen leader Ramzan Kadyrov in Chimaev's corner; Robert Whittaker predicts a Chimaev steamroll. No confirmed injuries or withdrawals, with recent form—Chimaev's unanimous decision title win over Dricus du Plessis and Strickland's February KO of Anthony Hernandez—sharpening focus on stylistic edges and endurance tests.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Nicolás Maduro as Venezuela's leader by end-2026 at 62.5% implied probability, reflecting persistent UN recognition of his status despite his January 3 US capture in Operation Absolute Resolve and ongoing New York detention awaiting trial. Delcy Rodríguez, Maduro's longtime vice president sworn in as interim president on January 5, commands 23.5% amid PSUV continuity, military command changes, and efforts to tighten control against internal divisions and Trump administration pressure for transition talks. Opposition figure María Corina Machado holds 7.5% following April unification behind her candidacy and pledges for a year-end return, though National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez's February rejection of new elections sustains regime dominance absent a snap vote or no-confidence trigger.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 72.5% implied probability for U.S. enactment of an AI safety bill before 2027, driven by the Trump administration's March 2026 National Policy Framework prioritizing AI innovation, federal preemption of state laws, and light-touch sector-specific rules over comprehensive safety regulations. In the divided 119th Congress, key proposals like Sen. Marsha Blackburn's TRUMP AMERICA AI Act discussion draft—focusing on child protections and copyrights—and Sen. Elissa Slotkin's AI Guardrails Act limiting Department of Defense AI use remain in early committee stages without floor votes. Recent bipartisan efforts target niche issues like chatbot restrictions for minors, but GOP splits on job impacts and a short legislative runway through year-end diminish prospects for passage amid administration signals against heavy-handed oversight.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US unemployment held steady at 4.3% in March 2026, edging down from 4.4% in February amid 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains that beat subdued expectations, signaling labor market resilience despite cooling job growth. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution, pricing in a potential peak around mid-single digits as GDP growth forecasts moderate to 1.8% and private-sector hiring slows. Key drivers include persistent wage pressures near 3.4% annually, Fed funds rate trajectory post-rate cuts, and softening job openings per unemployed worker below 1.0. Watch the April employment report due May 2, alongside May FOMC deliberations and Q1 GDP release, for signals on recession risks or further disinflation that could cap or elevate the 2026 peak.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 27% implied probability to win the 2026 WNBA championship after re-signing champions Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu, and Jonquel Jones while adding three-time All-Star Satou Sabally in free agency, bolstering their contending core amid a wide-open field. Indiana Fever sit at 16% following Aliyah Boston's four-year, $6.3 million extension and Kelsey Mitchell's supermax deal, fueling optimism around Caitlin Clark's sophomore leap and young backcourt firepower. Las Vegas Aces hold steady near 15.5% with A'ja Wilson's monster re-signing despite trading Kelsey Plum, while Atlanta Dream's 11.5% reflects aggressive offseason moves elevating their Eastern Conference standing. Minnesota Lynx faded to 9% post-draft as rivals strengthened rosters.

markets.question: Will FC Mito Holly Hock win on 2026-05-06?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 6, 2026
If FC Mito Holly Hock wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Kashima Antlers vs. FC Mito Holly Hock
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J1 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 6, 2026 between Kashima Antlers and FC Mito Holly Hock.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kashima Antlers hold a slim edge as J1 League table-toppers with 32 points from 13 matches, bolstered by home advantage at Kashima Soccer Stadium and a dominant historical head-to-head record (14 wins to Mito HollyHock's 1), yet trader consensus reflects caution after their 1-1 draw in the April 4 reverse fixture where Mito's compact defense neutralized Kashima's attack. Kashima's recent 2-1 loss to Tokyo Verdy snapped momentum from four wins in five, while sixth-placed Mito (16 points) shows mixed away form but resilience, contributing to the tight 43.5% Kashima, 32% draw, and 28% Mito HollyHock probabilities amid no major new injury concerns.

markets.question: Will The Deep die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

eventMetadata.context_description: As The Boys' fifth and final season unfolds on Prime Video—now past its April 8 premiere with Episode 5 airing last week—trader sentiment hinges on confirmed on-screen deaths that have already resolved parts of this high-stakes market. A-Train met his end in the opener via Homelander, a "really hard call" per showrunner Eric Kripke, while Firecracker's betrayal-fueled demise by the same supe in Episode 5 underscores the escalating body count Kripke likened to Survivor eliminations. Karl Urban teased massive casualties before Episode 7's credits, amplifying uncertainty for survivors like Billy Butcher, Homelander, and The Deep amid fan leaks. With three episodes left through the May 20 finale, late twists remain possible in this narrative-driven bloodbath.

markets.question: Will Michael Echols be the Republican nominee for LA-05?

eventMetadata.context_description: Blake Miguez leads trader consensus at 45.5% implied probability to win the Republican nomination for Louisiana's open 5th Congressional District seat, vacated by Rep. Julia Letlow's Senate bid, driven by President Trump's early February endorsement, dominant fundraising with nearly $4 million cash-on-hand reported mid-April, and consistent polling edges in recent surveys like the April Rainey Center poll showing him at 23% to Michael Echols' 20%. Echols holds second at 21.6% on strong local backing as a Monroe state representative and shared two-man race dynamics. Odds reflect a fragmented field likely headed to a June runoff under closed primary rules, tempered by Gov. Jeff Landry's recent suspension of May 16 House primaries following a Supreme Court map ruling, with redistricting revisions underway this week creating procedural uncertainty. An April 24 candidate debate underscored policy contrasts without major shifts.

markets.question: LoL: Hanwha Life Esports vs DN SOOPers - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Hanwha Life Esports and DN SOOPers in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 6 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports win Game 1 against DN SOOPers.

This market will resolve to "DN SOOPers" if DN SOOPers win Game 1 against Hanwha Life Esports.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Hanwha Life Esports vs DN SOOPers (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Hanwha Life Esports and DN SOOPers in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 6 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports win the match against DN SOOPers.

This market will resolve to "DN SOOPers" if DN SOOPers win the match against Hanwha Life Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hanwha Life Esports enters this LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 series as a perennial contender with a stable top-lane focused roster led by seasoned veterans, showing strong early-season adaptation to the latest patch meta emphasizing mid-game skirmishes and objective control. DN SOOPers, a rising squad from recent promotions, relies on aggressive bot-lane duo play but struggles with consistency in group stage matches against elite teams. No major roster changes, injuries, or scandals reported in the past 30 days; trader consensus reflects HLE's superior head-to-head history and home crowd advantage at LoL Park. Key watchpoints include draft phase picks on contested champions like Azir and Ornn, plus map 1 momentum in this closely contested matchup.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding victory from pole at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix, his third consecutive win after China and Japan, has extended his drivers' championship lead to 100 points over Mercedes teammate George Russell's 80, solidifying trader consensus at 40.4% and 26.5% implied probabilities respectively. Mercedes' early-season dominance, with consistent podiums and superior race pace, has positioned the duo as frontrunners amid Red Bull and McLaren struggles—Max Verstappen languishes outside the top five while Lando Norris holds third in markets at 11.1% despite fourth in standings. Recent grid penalties and Ferrari's post-Miami woes for Charles Leclerc further cap challengers, though long-term consistency and evolving regulations loom as variables.

markets.question: Will Moldova be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland leads Polymarket's trader consensus for a Eurovision 2026 top 3 finish at 71% implied probability, propelled by "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen dominating the recent OGAE fan poll and exhibiting strong jury appeal through pre-party showcases and Eurojury results. Greece follows at 56% with Akylas's edgy "Ferto" gaining traction for its televote potential and Balkan voting bloc support, while Denmark (42%) and France (42%) vie for podium spots amid staging critiques in initial rehearsals. First rehearsals in Vienna have introduced volatility, with early footage highlighting vocal powerhouses but underscoring risks in live execution. Semi-finals on May 12 and 14 precede the May 16 grand final, where jury-televote splits and home-crowd energy could spark upsets in this 35-entry field.

markets.question: Will Thomas Welby win the 2026 Galway West by-election?

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 760-779 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will JD Gaming qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.
